title: Patagonium oresigenum
taxonomy: Patagonium oresigenum is a genus of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the ice plant family, specifically within the subfamily Ruschioideae. This plant is a member of the genus Patagonium, which is part of the larger family Aizoaceae. The classification of Patagonium oresigenum places it within the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is native to the Americas and has been introduced to various other regions. The specific classification of Patagonium oresigenum provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants within the Aizoaceae family.
uses: Patagonium oresigenum is a species of flowering plant native to South America, but very little is known about its medicinal uses.
